title: Fab City Dataset Entry
description: Canonical schema for a single dataset entry in the awesome-fabcity-data list. Each entry lives at data/{pillar}/{scale}/{slug}.yaml and is validated against this schema in CI.

name.description: Human-readable canonical name for the dataset / source.

url.description: Primary URL — landing page or registry entry.

description.description: One- to two-sentence summary. What is it; what does it carry; who runs it.

pillar.description: Which Vivanco Full-Stack Metrics pillar this source primarily feeds.

scale.description: The scale tier where the source publishes its strongest signal. Some sources span scales; pick the one where the resolution is highest.

status.description: Operational status. live = current public read; stale = older than 12 months without update; paywalled = requires payment for full access; deprecated = officially retired; planned = upstream announced but not yet shipped.

license.description: License of the data itself, not the codebase. Use SPDX where possible (CC-BY-4.0, ODbL-1.0, CC0-1.0, MIT). Free-text OK if the source is multi-license.

api.description: Direct programmatic endpoint, if one exists. Null if download-only.

pilot_relevance.description: Which PLANETAI pilots (or 'global') this source has meaningful coverage for. Empty array means relevant elsewhere but not in current pilots.

tags.description: Free-form lowercase-hyphenated tags. Examples: air-quality, citizen-science, open-hardware, material-flow, procurement.

added.description: ISO date the entry was added to this list.

updated.description: ISO date of the last review of this entry's status / metadata.

wired_in_planetai.description: True if a connector for this source is currently live in the PLANETAI observatory.

upstream_listed_in.description: Other awesome-style lists or registries that already index this source. Helps avoid duplication of curation labour.

notes.description: Longer-form notes: API quirks, rate limits, licensing edge cases, recommended usage patterns, citation requirements.
